trigger: Before authoring a mission package or W0 brief; when 4+ clusters in a CF chain accumulate

codified_at_milestone: v1.49.638 + v1.49.654 (forward-cadence lessons appended); v1.49.910 (PARTIAL-backfill: #10364 SPS cohort-uniqueness audit + #10365 scaffold-manifest hint validation + #10401 brief title-line trip-vocab budget — documented in docs/MISSION-PACKAGE-DISCIPLINE.md; docs/scaffold-manifest-discipline.md registered as canonical doc this ship); v1.49.911 (UNCODIFIED-drain: #10366 mission-brief historical-record assertions marked preliminary; verify — documented in docs/MISSION-PACKAGE-DISCIPLINE.md)

trigger: Writing or modifying vitest tests; perf-assertion authoring; flake debugging; designing audit-record-count assertions for chokepoint-gated reads; authoring fake-fixture wire tests for chokepoint verification
summary: Audit-method conventions (hookTimeout, ORDER-BY tiebreaker, perf-warmup, skip-guard env-vars) + CF closure-verification templates + flake catalog + meta-test skip-guards against gitignored runtime artifacts + perf-assertion regex coverage + plain-bullet lesson format. Audit-record-count assertion for chokepoint-gated reads (#10456): when a chokepoint-gated method has derived methods that transitively call it, the integration test MUST assert exactly N audit records under N invocations — load-bearing regression detector against silent fidelity reductions (e.g., a future caching refactor that memoizes the gated method would silently reduce audit emissions, breaking observability without breaking unit tests). Three variants: two-site outer-loop (v892 dacp/bus/scanner.ts, 9 records under 1 outer + 8 inner-loop), derived-method ripple (v896 workflow-run-store.ts, 3 records under 3 derived-method calls), mixed read/write derived methods (v897 scan-state-store.ts, 2 records under 2 derived methods that each call load+save; save between them emits 0). Anti-pattern: assertion of "at-least-1" instead of exact N (silent fidelity reductions pass). Anti-pattern: omitting the write-emits-zero assertion (accidental future gating of writes goes unnoticed). Anti-pattern: conflating emit-count with invocation-count when invocations are async fire-and-forget (substrate→calibration end-to-end uses count + net-polarity, not sequence). v892 + v896 + v897 three-instance evidence. Fake-fixture wire test pattern (#10458): when a wire test exercises a chokepoint check (ProcessContext / EgressContext / LoaderContext spawn / exec / read gate) but doesn't need real data, use a minimal fake-bytes fixture with the correct extension and magic-byte signature (typically 4 bytes — e.g., `fake.png` with PNG magic 0x89 0x50 0x4e 0x47, `fake.zip` / `fake.docx` with ZIP magic 0x50 0x4b 0x03 0x04, `fake.pdf` with `%PDF`, `fake.gz` with gzip magic 0x1f 0x8b 0x08 0x00). The chokepoint gate fires synchronously before the side-effecting operation — the fact that the operation would fail on the fake data never matters because the security throw happens first. Permissive-mode wire tests wrap the call in `.catch(() => {})` with an inline comment explaining that spawn-time errors are expected (the assertion is on audit-record emission, not processing outcome). Anti-pattern: constructing a real valid file when the chokepoint gate fires first (wastes ~10-100x LOC + adds dependency surface). Anti-pattern: skipping the magic-byte signature (zero-byte file triggers a different code branch, bypassing the chokepoint). Anti-pattern: real-file fixtures committed to the repo (bloat + maintenance debt; generate in beforeAll, clean up in afterAll). v872 fake.png (pic2html) + v874 fake.zip + v874 fake.docx (acquirer) three-instance evidence.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.585+ + v1.49.654 (meta-test + audit lessons appended); v1.49.899 (extended with #10456 audit-record-count assertion for chokepoint-gated reads from v892 + v896 + v897 three-instance evidence); v1.49.901 (extended with #10458 fake-fixture wire test pattern from v872 + v874 three-instance evidence)

trigger: Editing .claude/{skills,agents,hooks}/ paths; staging files into .planning/, .claude/, .archive/, artifacts/, .gsd/, .chipset/, .env, project-claude/hooks/.log/
summary: PreToolUse hook BLOCKS self-mod operations without SC_SELF_MOD=1 / SC_INSTALL_CALLER=project-claude / npm_lifecycle_event override. git-add-blocker.js BLOCKS protected-path staging without SC_FORCE_ADD=1. settings.json gsd-config-guard-protected; new hooks route via project-claude/. Mission packages never committed. Sub-agent destination-directory ambiguity propagates a protected-path bypass (#10367): when a brief specifies a deliverable destination ambiguously ('likely under www/ or docs/'), the sub-agent may pick the path requiring the strongest overrides (www/ with ALLOW_WWW_COMMIT=1) — mitigation is upstream of the hook: briefs MUST state each deliverable's destination AND commit pattern unambiguously (tracked docs/ ordinary commit vs published www/ empty-commit) so the sub-agent never guesses across a protected boundary.

trigger: Authoring W2/W3 sub-agent dispatch prompts; spawning parallel build agents; sizing token budgets per dispatch; embedding chapter-generation in NASA build dispatches
summary: Sub-agents lack SendMessage in toolkit (spawn-task-return-result, not continuous peers); ~60-70 tool-use ceiling per dispatch; bound dispatches to ~1 component / 30-50min; commit-between-deliverables discipline; chunked Write+Edit append pattern for >32K HTML deliverables; transient API errors recover via identical-prompt retry; 64K output-token cap requires JSON+HTML split; W3.5 chapter-gen via `run-with-pg.mjs refresh --fast --quiet` belongs in the dispatch deliverable list (v709 codification). Content-filter & dispatch-cadence lessons promoted from the NASA campaign (v911): positive-framing dispatch prompts and DON'T enumerate forbidden tokens — they self-replicate into output (#10406); the trip-vocab density budget applies to the prompt itself not just the brief (#10407); secondary-trip-vocab density audit (primary > 0 OR secondary > 5) selects Path B main-context hand-author preemptively (#10402, sibling of #10401); content-filter-safe phrasing — date-pair memorial form, engineering register, single memorial section (#10387); sub-agent content-filter mitigation via partial-completion salvage (#10383); dual-direction substrate-form hard-block in prompts (#10378); foreground-author full-rewrite-at-scale recovery (#10388); dispatch as a clean cadence alternative for non-conflicting concurrent work (#10369); shared filename manifest in concurrent dispatch prompts prevents coordination drift (#10385); per-mission single-dispatch rebuild template, ~1200-word brief / 13 deliverables / 28-36 tool uses (#10408)

trigger: Before executing any REVIEW ledger entry or planning artifact written as a sweep summary; when authoring audit rules and audit verdicts
summary: Per-file recon (~15min) precedes per-file code (verify scope, framing, prior-art). Audit rules classify by BEHAVIOR (e.g. `node:fs` import presence), not by filename pattern. Interface-conformance audits report a 3-way verdict: ALREADY CONFORMS / SHOULD CONFORM / INTENTIONALLY DIFFERENT (third verdict is load-bearing — prevents future re-flagging). REVIEW ledger fields distinguish filename-collisions (rename-scale) from class-name-collisions (merge-scale).
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.784 (new domain — codified from v1.49.780-782 lesson cluster)

trigger: Introducing a chokepoint, interface, or generator-template to N existing modules with high call-site multiplicity; authoring a generator that may run on partial input; adding the SECOND instance of a class-typed family
summary: Optional `ctx?` parameter pattern for chokepoint retrofit — three states (undefined→legacy permissive; defaultLoaderContext()→audit-only; restricted→enforced); zero call-site churn. Tolerant generators emit skip-the-line over `UNKNOWN`/`TODO`/placeholder sentinels — placeholders pollute output, break round-trip, and mask the schema gap. Exception: emit the default when the field has a load-bearing default value. Cross-class registries extract at the SECOND class instance (not first, not third) — the contrast between two real instances determines the registry shape; deferring to the third class ships a temporary wrong-source measure that has to be unwound. Production-caller scope-reduction via path-narrowing (#10440): when a forward-flag names a wrapper class but the underlying path is directly callable, call the path directly — wrapper-class instantiation accretes peer-dependency surface that is not load-bearing for the path under test. Size-ascending chip-pick reveals wire-shape diversity (#10444): when chipping down a KNOWN_UNWIRED allowlist, pick entries smallest-LOC first — file size correlates with structural complexity, so the size-ascending traversal incidentally surfaces the catalog of viable wire shapes (hoist-at-top → hoist-outside-Promise → internal-helper #10433 → DI-executor #10441 → two-site hoisted-check) without any explicit variant-coverage planning. Anti-pattern: picking the highest-LOC or highest-risk file first forces the most complex wire shape at campaign start and buries the simple shapes. Spawn-site count (N) as primary wire-shape predictor (#10445): LOC is the coarse first-pass sort; N is the actual shape selector — high-LOC files with N=1 take hoist-at-top regardless of LOC band (v872 311 LOC + v875 1440 LOC both N=1, both hoist-at-top). Router-with-conditional-bypass (#10447): when a router branches between gated and non-gated paths (e.g. installFromLocal vs installFromRemote), thread `ctx?` only into the gated branch; leave the bypass branch's signature unchanged — chokepoint scope is a property of the destination, not the router. Shared-helper hoist sub-variant catalog (#10448): five sub-variants from the v868-v882 12-chip campaign — hoist-at-top / two-site hoisted-check / class-instance two-site / internal-helper-method / module-internal-helper — plus a `module-singleton` carry-forward (1 instance v881, below promotion bar). Sub-variant selection is N-driven (cross-ref #10445); pick from the catalog rather than force-fitting. Class-stored hoist-at-top sub-variant of #10448 (#10455): when the chip file is a class with EXACTLY ONE fs-op method (N=1 class-method), accept `ctx?` via constructor, store as `private readonly ctx?` instance field, hoist `this.ctx` at the top of the single fs-op method OUTSIDE the ENOENT-tolerant try/catch per #10442. Distinct from class-instance two-site (N≥2 methods each hoist independently) and from module-function hoist-at-top (preserves public method signature unchanged because ctx flows through constructor, not per-call). v890 calibration-adjustment-store + v896 workflow-run-store + v897 scan-state-store three-instance evidence. Class-multi-method consolidated-gate sub-variant of #10448 (#10459): when the chip file is a class wrapping a directory/namespace scope with M public read methods (M≥1) fanning into N private fs-op methods, hoist ensureAllowed ONCE at the top of each public read method on the class-wrapped scope; private fs-ops inherit the gate transitively (one audit per public call regardless of internal fan-out — cross-ref #10456 exact-N assertion). Audit target is the wrapped SCOPE — distinct from #10455's file-path target (N=1 single fs-op method) and from the class-instance multi-method per-file target (N≥2 parallel public methods on this.filePath). Mixed-mode methods gate the EXTERNAL path (read-from-external, v908 ingestSessionLog) or inherit out-of-scope (read-then-write internal, v907 get()). v902 state-reader (M=1) + v907 file-store (M=5) + v908 conversation-store (M=3+1 mixed) three-instance evidence.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.784 (new domain — codified from v1.49.782 LoaderContext + v1.49.783 normalizer fix lesson cluster); v1.49.802 (extended with #10426 second-instance cross-class registry extraction from T1.1 arc); v1.49.847 (extended with #10440 production-caller path-narrowing from v845 + v846 two-instance evidence); v1.49.868 (extended with #10444 size-ascending chip-pick reveals wire-shape diversity from v858-v862 Process cluster + v863-v867 Egress cluster 10-chip two-cluster evidence); v1.49.883 (extended with #10445 spawn-site count as primary wire-shape predictor from v872 + v875 two-instance evidence + #10447 router-with-conditional-bypass from v864 + v880 two-instance evidence + #10448 shared-helper hoist sub-variant catalog with Track 5 wire-shape table from v868-v882 12-chip cluster); v1.49.899 (extended with #10455 class-stored hoist-at-top sub-variant of #10448 from v890 + v896 + v897 three-instance evidence); v1.49.910 (extended with #10459 class-multi-method consolidated-gate sub-variant of #10448 from v902 + v907 + v908 three-instance evidence — counter-cadence codify ship)

trigger: When a handoff lists a fix as 'escalated' or 'load-bearing'; when a test in `tests/integration/` has been red across 2+ consecutive ships; when operator memory accumulates workarounds for a known broken tool
summary: Close escalated wedges within 1-2 milestones; do not defer beyond that. The cost of deferral is silent test drift + workaround documentation accumulation, not the eventual fix. Forward-cadence engine-state work can wait one milestone; an open red test in `tests/integration/` cannot. The pre-tag-gate's silent acceptance of a known-failing test is the alarm.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.784 (new domain — codified from v1.49.783 STATE.md normalizer fix)

trigger: Authoring or extending a CLI tool in `tools/` that scans the codebase, emits metrics, or produces a comparable report; designing its test harness; piping its output to another command
summary: Test harnesses use spawnSync (not execSync) so stderr survives exit 0; cross-module analyzers scan multiple importer roots (not just src/); metric-emitting tools commit a baseline file so future runs diff via git; CLIs flush stdout before process.exit() to avoid 64KB pipe-buffer truncation; diff-emitting observability tools document the warm-up period (first run has no prior baseline); version-stamped baseline writers refuse to overwrite existing files with different content unless --force (v794 closure of v791 trip). #10421 field-validated at v789 by adoption-baseline diff emitting exactly the predicted line. Static-analysis tool parsers must handle common code-shape variants OR fail loudly (#10450): two real-world bugs in the same tool — v867 regex terminator inside comments (false negative) + v885 alias-stripping in named-import extractor (false positive). Sibling of #10427 failure-mode contracts: tools surfacing silent failures must not themselves fail silently. Mitigating discipline: sanity-fixtures exercise aliased imports / brackets-in-comments / namespace imports / dynamic imports / re-exports; inline assertions compare structural-view count against ground-truth count.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.790 (new domain — codified from v1.49.785-789 adoption-telemetry build chain lesson cluster); v1.49.794 (extended with #10424 refuse-to-overwrite guard); v1.49.886 (extended with #10450 tool-parser-must-fail-loudly from v867+v885 two-instance evidence)

trigger: Authoring a per-module shelfware verdict (WIRED / RETIRED / ALLOWLISTED) for a module flagged by the adoption-scan as test-only; selecting the wire site that satisfies a WIRED verdict
summary: Observability surface (scanner + dashboard + allowlist) and decision surface (per-module verdicts) MUST live in separate files and evolve independently. Default to the lightest wire that flips the adoption-scan classification — a CLI subcommand usually suffices and preserves HARD-preservation invariants — defer richer in-loop wires to follow-on ships only if the verdict explicitly requires production use.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.790 (new domain — codified from v1.49.789 first shelfware verdict lesson cluster)

trigger: Designing or extending the bounded-learning calibration loop in `src/bounded-learning/`; choosing the statistical primitive for an anytime-valid threshold-update decision; adding a new calibratable threshold class
summary: Two-sided likelihood-ratio e-processes on bounded binary observations at |x|=1 are insensitive to unanimous direction (cosh(λ)·exp(−λ²/2) ≤ 1 for all λ ≥ 0); use two one-sided e-processes at Bonferroni α/2 instead. Math-check-before-test-fixture convention: compute E_n/E_{n-1} under unanimous evidence by hand before writing the trip-point assertion — if the ratio is ≤ 1 the construction is unfit. Per-class observation sources are necessary surface; the lightest-wire discipline does NOT apply to necessary surface (cross-ref Architecture-retrofit patterns #10426). Calibrate-axis read-side wire recipe (#10451): 7-step pattern applied at v837 (predictive) + v884 (observation-retention) + v888 (token_budget.max_percent) — new events module mirror, dispatcher branch + wired-flag flip, public-API exports with alias if collision, CLI manual recorder dispatch + recorder function, read-side tests (~13), dispatcher tests with wired-flag flip + round-trip, CLI --summary count bump. STABLE at v888 (3 instances). Polarity convention matches threshold semantics (raise reduces fire-frequency → +1 favors lower); inverted for thresholds where raise INCREASES fire-frequency (predictive). Substrate-wrapper pattern (#10452): the substrate write-side ship follows a thin-function shape (`runX` function + minimal config interface + three-flag options bag + fire-and-forget auto-emit per #10437). Two sub-variants — default-fixed (v891 retention: substrate returns work-count, polarity is operator-intent, default kind is conservative bias) and outcome-driven (v893 ceiling-check: substrate IS a comparison, polarity falls out of the result). Discriminator: does the substrate's natural output determine polarity? Sibling of #10451 — #10451 is the read-side procedure, #10452 is the write-side procedure.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.802 (new domain — codified from v1.49.795 T1.1 ship 1 e-process design lesson); v1.49.886 (extended with #10451 read-side wire recipe from v837+v884 two-instance evidence); v1.49.895 (extended with #10452 substrate-wrapper write-side pattern from v891+v893 two-instance evidence + #10451 STABLE promotion at v888 3rd instance)

trigger: Designing a new surface (function, module, CLI flag, skill prompt section, hook) where silent-vs-loud failure asymmetry is in play; adding a `try { ... } catch { /* ignore */ }` block; reviewing a long-running primitive's transient-error handling; adding a subscriber-gated observability hook on a context/options type
summary: Forensic, dashboard, and observability surfaces fail silently; load-bearing surfaces fail loudly. Test: does the user's next decision depend on this surface's output? If yes → load-bearing → propagate. If no → accessory → swallow at surface boundary. Contract documentation convention: explicit `// best-effort silent` or `// fail loudly` marker in docstring paired with at least one test assertion that exercises the failure path; surfaces without the paired test are stochastic. Subscriber-gated observability-only context-hook pattern (#10437): substrate-consumer hooks recur in a 5-element shape (optional `?` field + subscriber gate + fire-and-forget Promise + .catch(() => {}) swallow + two-argument derived-data signature). PAIR co-location (when multiple hooks live on the same surface) shares one try/catch to prevent per-hook drift. Re-throw ProcessContextDenied from CLI swallow-catch (#10442): CLI surfaces that absorb ALL errors into structured JSON output must re-throw `ProcessContextDenied` as the first line of the catch block — security denials are load-bearing per #10427 and must propagate even when operational errors are absorbed for UX. Multi-catch helper (#10446): when a chokepoint campaign chips many call sites across both ProcessContext AND EgressContext (~30 instances by v882), the per-catch `if (err instanceof XContextDenied) throw err;` boilerplate consolidates into one helper — `rethrowIfDenied(err)` (inline form for catch blocks with bespoke reporting) or `callOrRethrowDenial(fn)` (higher-order wrapper for catch blocks that collapse to `undefined`). One helper, one source of truth for which denials propagate; future class additions (LoaderContextDenied) update one place. Fire-and-forget test-side wait via `setTimeout(50ms)` (#10454): when asserting on a fire-and-forget Promise's side effects, the test-side wait MUST use a real timeout (50ms is enough for typical mkdir + appendFile on real disk), NOT `setImmediate(resolve)` or `process.nextTick` — those advance only the event loop, while OS-level fs operations require wall-clock time. v891 + v893 + v894 three-instance evidence. Limitation: the `.catch(() => {})` swallow is correct-by-construction; tests can't reliably trigger the failure path without OS-level setup, so the protection is structural rather than test-verified.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.802 (new domain — codified from v1.49.799-801 three-instance audit-log + watch-loop + /sc:status lesson cluster); v1.49.840 (extended with #10437 subscriber-gated context-hook pattern from v810+v826 onPredictions + v830+v832 fallbackProvider four-instance evidence set + PAIR co-location refinement); v1.49.847 (extended with #10442 re-throw ProcessContextDenied from CLI swallow-catch from v820 + v842 two-instance evidence); v1.49.883 (extended with #10446 multi-catch helper from ~30-instance evidence across v868-v882 Track 4+5 chokepoint clusters); v1.49.895 (extended with #10454 fire-and-forget test-side wait pattern from v891+v893+v894 three-instance evidence)

trigger: Planning a counter-cadence cleanup-mission; deciding which operational debt to spend a non-degree-advancing ship on; auditing whether the project's four operational axes (codify / consume / calibrate / verify) are in balance
summary: Four operational axes — codify (~7-10 ships), consume (≤6 ships from substrate to first non-test caller), calibrate (~10 ships per wired threshold tick), verify (~10 ships per substrate-with-callers-but-no-integration-test; #10438 promoted at v847 from v829+v832 evidence). Cadence-overdue check is prose-only and informational: each axis names its overdue trigger; the operator picks; the next counter-cadence's scope discussion NAMES the proposed axis even if a different axis wins on operator priority. CLI manual + substrate auto-emit duality (#10439): a calibratable threshold's loop is structurally incomplete until BOTH write callers ship — a CLI manual recorder (operator-attributed events with explicit polarity) AND a substrate auto-recorder (traffic-attributed events with a default polarity that mirrors the CLI default). Plan THREE ships per threshold: observation-source registration → CLI manual recorder → substrate auto-recorder. Substrate→calibration end-to-end integration test pattern (#10453): the verify-axis closing-move test shape — temp dir setup, substrate write, fire-and-forget wait via setTimeout(50ms) per #10454, calibration-loop read, polarity assertion (single + multi-event with net-sum check), missing-file tolerance, malformed-line tolerance. ESTABLISHED at v1.49.899 with v856 predictive low-confidence + v894 observation-retention + v898 token-budget-max-percent three-instance evidence. Substrate-specific variation axes documented in canonical doc: sync vs async substrate, inverse vs default-fixed vs outcome-driven kind selection, boundary cases, override mechanisms, multi-event ordering preservation. v898 surfaced two sub-disciplines: sync-substrate fire-and-forget order non-determinism (assertions on multi-call accumulated state MUST be order-independent — count + net-polarity, not sequence) + outcome-driven kind subtlety (test both polarities from one substrate-API surface). Mirror v856 / v894 / v898 as the skeleton when filing the integration-test ship; verify-axis budget extends 10 ships from substrate ship version.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.805 (new domain — codified from S3 lever in 2026-05-26 core-functions audit retrospective); v1.49.844 (verify-axis added as canonical-doc home for v829+v832 evidence base); v1.49.847 (numbered-lesson promotions: #10438 verify axis + #10439 CLI manual + substrate auto-emit duality from v803 + v845/v846 evidence); v1.49.895 (extended with #10453 substrate→calibration end-to-end integration test pattern from v856+v894 two-instance evidence); v1.49.899 (#10453 promoted to ESTABLISHED at v898 3rd instance + variation-axis table from outcome-driven sync-substrate evidence + two sub-disciplines surfaced)

trigger: Authoring docs that surface opt-in flags to operators; reviewing whether a new substrate ship needs a public-surface entry; deciding what a new operator's second-30-minutes path should look like
summary: Per-substrate four-field shape: what it unlocks / what it costs / opt-in mechanic / when to defer. Public surface (this doc) is the WHY-truth source; `docs/MODULE-DEFAULTS.md` is the flag-truth source. Add new entries below their axis grouping (observability / learning / execution / safety) in ship order. Retire entries only on codebase removal, not on default-flip (note inline). Cross-link to the canonical flag-truth doc; do NOT duplicate flag tables.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.805 (new domain — codified from S4 lever in 2026-05-26 core-functions audit retrospective)

trigger: Adding a new src/ caller that reads from disk, issues a network request, or spawns a child process; reviewing whether a new module needs to thread an existing `ctx?` parameter; migrating a KNOWN_UNWIRED entry from one of the audit-test allowlists
summary: Three sibling chokepoints — LoaderContext (filesystem, v782), EgressContext (network egress, v806), ProcessContext (child-process spawn, v806) — share a common shape (optional `ctx?` param, allow-list pattern, audit sink, `*ContextDenied` error) but specialize their targets. Each chokepoint's audit-test (`src/security/*-context-audit.test.ts`) is the runtime enforcement: greps src/ for the surface's signature, accepts either `ensure*Allowed(` calls or `Role: NOT a <surface> caller` docstring exemption, grandfathered existing call sites tracked in KNOWN_UNWIRED. Security denials are load-bearing per #10427 — hoist `ensure*Allowed(` OUTSIDE try/catch blocks that swallow errors. Internal-helper pattern (#10433): when a file already wraps the side-effecting op in an internal helper, thread `ctx?` through the helper for 1 LOC × N callsites; files without a helper take N LOC × N callsites. DI-executor + tokenized-argv wire shape (#10441): when a module exposes a factory with an optional injected executor for testability, the default executor closes over `ctx?`, tokenizes the cmd string to extract executable + argv, and calls `ensureProcessAllowed` before delegating — a sub-class of #10433 where the default executor IS the internal helper. execFile vs shell-exec audit target accuracy (#10449): direct-exec (`execFile`) records the actual binary in the audit log; shell-exec (`exec`) records `sh`. Prefer direct-exec for new ProcessContext wires when no shell features (pipes, globs, redirection, variable expansion) are required — tighter allow-list (admits only the named binary class), greppable audit records, narrower blast radius from over-grants. Tokenize cmd strings with `trim().split(/\s+/)` per #10441's tokenize step. Read-side-only chokepoint at write-bearing classes (#10457): LoaderContext is a READ-side chokepoint by design — when a wired class has BOTH read and write methods, only the read methods are gated; the write methods are intentionally out-of-scope (LoaderContext's docstring scopes the disk-read surface; gating writes would require a separate WriterContext that doesn't exist). Test the discipline explicitly with a not-gated-write assertion (assert restricted-ctx call succeeds AND emits zero audit records); without it, accidental future gating of writes goes unnoticed. Cross-cuts #10455 (class-stored hoist-at-top wire shape) + #10456 (audit-record-count test). v890 calibration-adjustment-store (save not gated) + v896 workflow-run-store (append not gated) + v897 scan-state-store (save not gated) three-instance evidence.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.806 (new domain — codified from S6 lever in 2026-05-26 core-functions audit retrospective; extends #10414 LoaderContext pattern to network egress + child-process spawn surfaces); v1.49.824 (extended with #10433 internal-helper pattern for ctx? threading from v809 + v820 case studies); v1.49.847 (extended with #10441 DI-executor + tokenized-argv wire shape from v825 + v843 three-instance evidence); v1.49.883 (extended with #10449 execFile vs shell-exec audit target accuracy from v853 + v874 two-instance evidence); v1.49.899 (extended with #10457 read-side-only chokepoint at write-bearing classes from v890 + v896 + v897 three-instance evidence)

trigger: Designing a closure for a drift class that originates from a multi-step operator procedure OR from a tool blindly overwriting destination files that may contain hand-authored content; reviewing whether a one-layer fix (detector OR source-eliminator alone) is structurally complete; planning a counter-cadence ship targeting an operational-debt class
summary: Procedure-rooted drift classes (where an operator-discretion step is forgettable/skippable) require BOTH a source eliminator (deterministic tool replacing the discretion step + post-condition check) AND a detector gate (structural check catching bypass). Either layer alone is incomplete: source-without-detector lets future operators work around the tool; detector-without-source leaves the human-error window open. Case study: STATE.md v805→v806 drift closed in v807 (detector: pre-tag-gate step 0.5) + v813 (source: atomic-writer `tools/state-md-set-shipped.mjs`). Generalizes to file-overwrite drift (#10436): v836 publish.mjs `shouldPublishToDestination` is the destination-side preservation layer paired with chapter.mjs's source-side preservation (from v585 C04). The manual recovery procedure operators worked around for 251 ships (`git checkout HEAD -- <chapters>`) was the alarm bell for a missing destination-side detector. Apply at every counter-cadence ship targeting a known procedure-rooted or file-overwrite drift class.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.814 (new domain — codified from v807+v813 case study; extends #10414 gate-not-vigilance to procedure-rooted drift specifically); v1.49.840 (extended with #10436 file-overwrite drift sub-class from v813 STATE.md + v836 publish.mjs two-instance evidence); v1.49.910 (PARTIAL-backfill: #10373 STATE.md normalizer drift recurrence closure — the v807+v813 case study's recurrence-soak lesson, documented in tools/pre-tag-gate.sh step 0.5)

trigger: Introducing a new audit-test that enforces a cross-cutting invariant on src/ files with grandfathered call sites; reviewing how to grandfather existing call sites without permanently exempting them; planning chip-down ships against an existing allowlist; consolidating allowlist entries when N siblings are all wired; adding inverse-audit coverage for a newly-surfaced stale-entry shape
summary: When a chokepoint or cross-cutting audit-test is introduced into a mature codebase, it MUST grandfather existing call sites via a named allowlist in the audit-test itself. The allowlist is debt, not exemption — should asymptote toward zero as chip ships consume it. Per-ship release notes record `<surface> KNOWN_UNWIRED N → N-K`. Established pattern at v806 (initial 16 egress + 38 process allowlist entries) + v809+v811+v812 (3 chip ships demonstrating the migration cadence). Generalization beyond chokepoints (#10434): the same ratchet-ledger shape works for any cross-cutting observability+enforcement surface where the current state has N enumerated out-of-conformance entries and each is independently chip-able — v821 discipline-coverage ceiling generalizes the chokepoint KNOWN_UNWIRED pattern to per-discipline UNCODIFIED counts. Inverse-audit stale-entry detection (#10443): the v806 audit-tests are unidirectional — they enforce 'files NOT in KNOWN_UNWIRED but containing the signature MUST call ensure*Allowed' but do NOT enforce the inverse. Two distinct stale-shapes have accumulated under that asymmetry — Shape A (wired-but-still-in-allowlist, surfaced v834) closed by v838 inline inverse-checks; Shape B (import-without-use, surfaced v852) closed by v857 cross-audit tool `tools/security/check-stale-known-unwired.mjs`. The discipline scales by adding one inverse-check per stale-shape; a third shape extends the catalog rather than creating a new discipline. Continuous-verification mode (refinement of #10443 operationalized v1.49.858-867): invoke the cross-audit tool after each chip ship as the third step in build → verify → ship — 10 consecutive applications across Process+Egress clusters surfaced the tool's first real-world parser bug at v867 (regex hardened with line-anchor + multi-line flag). Sibling discipline: tools designed to surface silent-vs-loud asymmetry must themselves fail loudly — a vacuous-true tool is worse than no tool. Add sanity-check assertions comparing tool's structural-view count against the actual allowlist file's enumerated entries.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.814 (new domain — codified from v806 introduction + v809+v811+v812 chip-ship demonstration; companion to Security chokepoints discipline); v1.49.824 (extended with #10434 ratchet-ledger generalization from v821 discipline-coverage ceiling case study); v1.49.857 (extended with #10443 inverse-audit stale-entry-detection from v834 Shape A + v852 Shape B two-instance evidence; cross-audit tool tools/security/check-stale-known-unwired.mjs); v1.49.868 (extended with #10443 continuous-verification mode operational discipline from v858-v867 10-consecutive-application evidence + v867 first-real-world-bug surfaced/fixed in-flight)

trigger: Authoring a wire between src/ and .college/ (or any future rootdir pair) where the two rootdirs cannot import each other directly; declaring duck-typed interfaces in one rootdir + satisfying structurally in the other; verifying the wire works end-to-end at the application boundary
summary: Cross-rootdir wires follow a 4-step pattern: (1) declare the interface in the CONSUMER rootdir with minimal primitive field types; (2) implement the satisfier in the PROVIDER rootdir via duck typing (no `implements` keyword; no cross-import); (3) bridge via an optional context field that's no-op when unset; (4) verify with a `tests/integration/*.integration.test.ts` test that has visibility into BOTH rootdirs. Two subordinate patterns: local-interface redeclaration discipline (when the satisfier RETURNS a typed value, the provider rootdir must locally redeclare the result type byte-equivalently — drift between the two declarations is silent), and `Pick<T, K>` structural-handle narrowing (when constructor opts accept concrete classes, type them as `Pick<>` so tests can pass thin mocks without a cast while real instances still flow through subtyping).
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.833 (new domain — codified from 5-instance evidence set v823 SkillActivationObserver declaration + v829 application-boundary verification + v830 ConceptFallbackProvider framework + v831 RosettaConceptFallback implementation + v832 selector 2nd-caller wire + tests/integration/ verification)

trigger: Authoring or rebuilding NASA degree-campaign mission pages; choosing direct-author vs sub-agent-dispatch vs hybrid cadence for same-day degree advances; tracking substrate-axis rotation / substrate-cohort observations; framing memorial or disaster content for content-filter safety
summary: Operational disciplines from the v652-v716 NASA degree-campaign mission-authoring effort (campaign frozen at degree 1.178 — a pressure-margin hold, not a cancellation; disciplines preserved for resume + a flagged general subset). Authoring cadence ladder — direct-author advances a degree at ~half depth / ~quarter wall-clock but loses the W1 correction safety-net (#10341); track consecutive-direct-author cluster length (1-2 low-risk, 3 consider dispatch, 4+ dispatch-required) (#10350); direct-author-with-focused-W2-dispatch hybrid meets depth gates at ~1.5-2h/degree, sustainable 4-8 consecutive (#10352); scale the counter-cadence response to debt breadth (#10374); long inter-flight gaps are scope-isolated work windows (#10376). Same-day cluster-length monitoring — flag near-term-verifiable predictions distinctly from long-horizon forward-shadows (#10348); 3/4-consecutive same-calendar-day degree thresholds (#10354, #10356); 4-consecutive threshold-hit triggers a counter-cadence cluster preemptively (#10371). Dispatch coordination — transient-API retry-first re-validation (#10268); 3-milestone infrastructure→content-1→content-2 trio closes 8+-page cross-track drift (#10271); name the root cause when mission numbering diverges from launch order (#10349). Substrate-axis rotation & stability — set explicit per-page byte/line targets in asymmetric uplifts (#10270); return to earlier categories after 3-5 pivots to avoid density saturation (#10345); declare axis rotations explicitly + hard-block predecessor-leak (#10381); intra-program continuation is sustained stability NOT a rotation, don't increment the count (#10394); one axis can host multiple functional subforms without rotation (#10395). Substrate-cohort/anchor observation accounting — anchor paired observations at one milestone (#10389); orbital-class multi-decade lifetimes are distinct from surface-class (#10397); cohorts can open with deferred realization (#10398); track anchor realizations cumulatively (#10399). Mission-brief accuracy — model intermediate states of multi-milestone forward references explicitly (#10250); document engineering-root-cause events as substrate-anchors at first observation (#10346). Content-filter & memorial framing (NASA-specific) — forbid meta-statements about the content policy itself, not just the forbidden content (#10269); explicit memorial-substrate-respect directives in dispatch prompts (#10380). Operator-directed — document operator-authorized departures from a lesson default as substrate-distinct (#10384); record operator-directed mid-cadence program-family pivots distinctly from routine axis rotations (#10390). The genuinely cross-cutting campaign lessons (content-filter phrasing, dispatch density, protected-path safety, brief accuracy) were promoted to their existing homes (Sub-agent dispatch / Self-modification safety / Mission package framing) and are cross-referenced in the canonical doc.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.911 (new domain — codified from the v1.49.652-716 NASA mission-authoring campaign UNCODIFIED lesson cluster; counter-cadence #12 codify-drain ship; 27 NASA-specific lessons here + 12 cross-cutting siblings promoted to Sub-agent dispatch / Self-modification safety / Mission package framing)
